title: Concurrency-capped parallel fan-out with conflict-aware aggregation
source: Search description: Anthropic multi-agent research system (lead-agent parallel subagents, bounded concurrency) + standard worker-pool semaphore pattern (live fetch blocked)
description: Team parallelism risks unbounded subprocess spawn and merge conflicts when Morty's touch overlapping files. A bounded worker pool (semaphore = min(cpu-2, configured cap)) plus the existing reserve_files gate prevents resource thrashing and write collisions, and a structured aggregation step gives Rick one clean rollup instead of N raw transcripts.
proposed_changes: In workers.py wrap parallel delegate calls in an asyncio.Semaphore (or ThreadPoolExecutor with max_workers from config). Before dispatch, call reserve_files for each worker's target paths and queue any worker whose files are already reserved instead of running it concurrently. Add an `aggregate_results(results)` function that returns a structured summary (per-ticket status, files changed, conflicts) for the team report, and emit a roro 'fanout_complete' event with the rollup.
